channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: House passes bill to fund federal agencies through September, though prospects unclear in Senate
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The House passed legislation Tuesday to avert a partial government shutdown and fund federal agencies through September, providing critical momentum as the measure now moves to the Senate, where bipartisan support will be needed to get it over the finish line.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: House Republicans pass bill to avert government shutdown
item.description: The House on Tuesday passed a funding bill to avert an end-of-the-week government shutdown, teeing up the measure for consideration in the Senate. The chamber cleared the continuing resolution (CR) in a largely party-line 217-213 vote, with just one Democrat — Rep.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Trump halts doubling of tariffs on Canadian metals, after Ontario suspends electricity price hikes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump 's threat on Tuesday to double his planned tariffs on steel and aluminum from 25% to 50% for Canada led the provincial government of Ontario to suspend its planned surcharges on electricity sold to the United States.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, ROB GILLIES and MICHELLE PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about Mahmoud Khalil, the Columbia protester arrested by ICE and facing deportation
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A Palestinian activist who helped lead student protests at Columbia University faces deportation after being arrested over the weekend by federal immigration officials.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Wall Street falls in a manic day after briefly dropping more than 10% below its record
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The U.S. stock market fell further Tuesday following President Donald Trump’s latest escalation in his trade war, briefly pulling Wall Street 10% below its record set last month.
item.dc:creator: STAN CHOE, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: White House pressures Columbia University as it seeks to deport pro-Palestinian activists
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The White House complained Tuesday that Columbia University is refusing to help federal agents find people being sought as part of the government’s effort to deport participants in pro-Palestinian demonstrations, as the administration continued to punish the school by yanking federal
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Metallica's epic Mexico City show comes to Apple Vision Pro, bringing viewers closer to their hits
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Metallica is bringing their powerhouse performance from Mexico City straight to viewers' faces courtesy of Apple. The company said Tuesday that Metallica's show will be delivered in an immersive concert experience on the Apple Vision Pro video headset on Friday.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN LANDRUM Jr., Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Portugal’s government loses confidence vote, triggering early election amid prolonged instability
item.description: LISBON, Portugal (AP) — Portugal’s minority government lost a confidence vote in parliament Tuesday, forcing its resignation and bringing the European Union country’s third general election in three years.
item.dc:creator: BARRY HATTON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Beth Holloway addresses missing American college student, urges 'full circle' safety plan
item.description: The mother of Natalee Holloway addresses the search for missing American college student Sudiksha Konanki in Punta Cana, Dominican Republic.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NOAA set to fire 1,029 more employees
item.description: Staffing levels at the nation's already hobbled weather, climate and oceans agency could take a further nosedive in the days ahead.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: How microplastics may be stoking antibiotic resistance
item.description: The microscopic shards of plastic found in every corner of the planet may be exacerbating antibiotic resistance, a new study has found.
item.dc:creator: Sharon Udasin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Education Department says 60 universities under investigation for antisemitism
item.description: The Department of Education’s Office of Civil Rights (OCR) is investigating 60 universities for antisemitism, according to letters sent on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, ABC 4

item.title: FBI joins search for US college student who vanished in Dominican Republic on spring break
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO, Dominican Republic (AP) — The FBI has joined the search for a 20-year-old University of Pittsburgh student who went missing nearly a week ago while on spring break in the Dominican Republic tourist town of Punta Cana, police said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NTSB makes 'urgent safety recommendations' after DC plane crash
item.description: NTSB investigators said there have been 15,214 "near-miss events" at DCA from Oct. 2021 to Dec. 2024.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Why you'll no longer see 'excessive heat warnings' in the forecast
item.description: It may be hard to think about sweltering temperatures in early March but excessive heat watches and warnings are getting a new name.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: Trump to increase steel and aluminum tariffs on Canada to 50 percent in response to electricity surcharge
item.description: The uncertainty around Trump's tariff plans have led to a downturn in the stock market, and the president has not ruled out the possibility of a recession this year.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Wall Street ends manic day modestly lower after briefly dropping more than 10% below its record
item.description: The U.S. stock market fell further Tuesday following President Donald Trump’s latest escalation in his trade war, briefly pulling Wall Street 10% below its record set last month. And like it's been most of the past few weeks, the market’s slide on Tuesday was erratic and dizzying.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump doubles planned tariffs on Canadian steel and aluminum to 50% as trade war intensifies
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump says that he will double his planned tariffs on steel and aluminum from 25% to 50% for Canada, escalating a trade war with the United States' northern neighbor.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: PHOTOS: 132-year-old wreck of 'one of the safest ships afloat' discovered in Lake Superior
item.description: The Great Lakes Shipwreck Historical Society has announced the discovery of a new wreck on Lake Superior: The SS Western Reserve.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, WGN 9

item.title: An office known for enforcing special education is now focused on Trump's political priorities
item.description: The Education Department’s Office for Civil Rights is best known for enforcing disability rights across America’s schools, but under President Donald Trump it’s being reshaped into a frontline figure in the president’s political battles.
item.dc:creator: Collin Binkley And Heather Hollingsworth, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Harvard freezes hiring amid financial 'uncertainties' driven by Trump policies
item.description: Harvard University says it is freezing hiring for faculty and staff amid financial “uncertainties” driven by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9

item.title: Greenland votes amid Trump invitation to join US: What to know
item.description: Greenland's upcoming national election could set the stage for a later referendum on its future control, as President Trump has renewed his quest to take over the natural resources-rich arctic land.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, Fox 5

item.title: Arizona prisoner set to be executed next week passes up chance to ask for reprieve
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — A prisoner scheduled to be executed next week in what would be Arizona’s first use of the death penalty in over two years has passed up a chance to ask for a reprieve from his death sentence.
item.dc:creator: JACQUES BILLEAUD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: X hit by 'massive cyberattack,' Musk says amid intermittent outages Monday
item.description: Elon Musk says there was a "massive cyberattack against X" on Monday amid several outages to the social media site.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Top US health agency makes $25,000 buyout offer to most of its 80,000 employees
item.description: The mass email went out to a “broad population of HHS employees," landing in their inboxes days before agency heads are due to offer plans for shrinking their workforces.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: South Carolina man called for end of death penalty before execution in final statement
item.description: "I want my closing statement to be one of love and a calling to my fellow Christians to help us end the death penalty," Brad Kieth Sigmon wrote ahead of his execution.
item.dc:creator: Kelci O'Donnell, WGN 9

item.title: Do you need fluoride after a teeth cleaning? Dental experts weigh in
item.description: Dentists and hygienists often propose fluoride treatments to patients in the dental chair to prevent cavities and strengthen teeth. But the relatively simple procedure – which involves applying a varnish, gel or foam directly to the teeth – isn’t always covered by insurance, especially for adults.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Texas district attorney declines to pursue charges against Kansas City Chiefs wide receiver Xavier Worthy
item.description: Xavier Worthy was arrested Friday and has since been released after the county's district attorney said he would not accept the case at this time.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Courts grapple with 'King' Trump
item.description: The White House sees few, if any, limits on President Trump's executive powers in his second term, but the federal court system is much less sure.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ohio couple charged after hundreds of living and dead rats found inside home
item.description: A Stark County couple is facing animal cruelty charges after authorities discovered horrific conditions inside their home, which included hundreds of living and dead rats.
item.dc:creator: Jack Shea, Fox 5

item.title: Judge awards nearly $2M after concluding FBI agent was negligent in shooting kidnapped Texas man
item.description: A federal judge has awarded nearly $2 million in damages as part of a civil lawsuit after concluding an FBI agent was negligent when he fatally shot a kidnapped Texas man during a botched rescue attempt in 2018.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5
